ENGNew: She's Really a Good Girl, You Know
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Really, She's a Good Girl"

TextENGNew: ``...But Chisato, is that really all right?``
``Huh, is what all right?``

``That promise you made earlier.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3003. I don't think it's too late even if you give it some more thought.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3004. Besides, you don't have to do this tomorrow. Couldn't you talk to her about it at school at the start of next week?``
Dad turned to face me with full attention as he asked that. But I didn't regret anything, so I answered cheerfully.

``Come on, that's no good.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3003. The counseling schedule will be announced on Monday, but there will be kids who start that same day.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3004. And Natsumi's a transfer student from the country, so she'll stick out for more than her grades.``
``And knowing that homeroom teacher's personality, I'm sure she'll be really fired up on the first day.``
``...Sounds like you have a lot to say about that homeroom teacher.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3006. I think the teacher's name was Ogata. Do you not like her?``
``Hmm, it's not like that, but...``

First off, it's not just Ogata. There are no really good teachers at my school. For me, who enrolled in this school just to be with Tamako and Akira, the expectations associated with being in an advanced school have been sickening.
It's fine that they rank the students. That's probably necessary for them to help us compete properly. But the way they force future decisions upon us isn't just excessive, it's tyrannical.

``Ogata-sensei has been in charge of career guidance since last year, but... she's the kind of teacher who is only interested in increasing the school's passing rate.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3008. She doesn't allow the students to be adventurous. She makes them aim for reliable places.``

``She all but ignores their career aspirations.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3010. According to our upperclassmen, she's been telling even the hardworking kids, ``At your level, your university choices are here and here,`` and drastically limiting them.``
``That sort of thing is tough for an earnest person like Natsumi.``
Honestly, Natsumi's grades are pretty average.
...But, I know the truth. That girl has been listening carefully and putting a ton of effort into taking notes to make up for the lag of transferring to a new school.
Because of that... I wanted to help her. I'll be damned if those obstinate teachers trample Natsumi's efforts just because her grades don't reflect them.

``Well, that may be so,@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3008. ...but didn't you have a concert tomorrow that you were looking forward to?@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3009. How many months back did you get the tickets? And you were talking about it last night, weren't you?``
``Ahahaha, ...is that right?@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3013. Well, this isn't the only time they'll be coming to Japan, so I'll just wait for my next chance.``
``Plus, Yumi was really depressed about not scoring a ticket, so now she'll owe me when I give it to her. Killing two birds with one stone, huh?@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3015. Mwahaha...!``
After saying that, I put a smile on my face to show that it's no problem.
...To be honest, I was looking forward to this concert more than Yumi. I'll probably never be lucky enough to get seats right in front of the stage again.
But... like I said, I don't regret anything.
No matter how big a fan I am, Natsumi is far more important. She's my highest priority. Because she's my best friend.
Really... to me, she's irreplaceable...
``By the way... is Natsumi-san doing all right?@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3011. When I first saw her, I was a little surprised.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3012. ...They're so alike.``
``...Is that so?@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3017. Hmm...``
``......@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3014.From what I've heard from Shinsuke, he didn't seem to think so.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3015. Their ages may be different, but...``
I glanced at Yamashina-san's reflection in the rearview mirror. ...Shinsuke is Yamashina-san's first name. He probably got that impression of Natsumi and mentioned it to Dad.
``It's unusual for you to be showing so much kindness to a girl you haven't known for long.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3017. ...Is that why?``
``...It's not like that, Dad.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3020. That girl... is a nice girl.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3021. That's all. Really... that's all.``
``...@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3019.I know how you feel. ...At least, I'd like to think I do.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3020. But, Chisato...``
This was getting a little out of hand.
I lashed out at him for prodding at things I didn't want touched. ...Being my father, that was enough for him to realize he'd rubbed me the wrong way.
``...Sorry.@k@vS20/00/VTT_saekiF3022. I shouldn't have said that.``
To escape the awkwardness, I turned to look at the flowing landscape out the window. ...And without saying anything, Dad opened his window and lit a cigarette.
``...That's right.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3025. That girl is Natsumi.@k@vS20/37/VTT_chisa3026. My dear, dear friend...``
I softly murmured to myself, and those words rolled out.
Suddenly, right then... in my mind, I saw that girl's smiling face with another face overlapping it, and a sigh escaped me...
